projectSummary: A small first-release web app that helps residents browse community workshops, request registration, and lets staff review and confirm signups in one local workflow.
assumptions: The first release focuses on workshop registration requests and staff review rather than payments or waitlists.
Only internal staff need access to the registration review surface in the initial MVP.
questions: Should the first release support a simple attendance status after confirmation, or is registration confirmation enough for MVP validation?
Does the community center need multilingual workshop content in the first release, or can English-only content validate the initial flow?

items.title: Coordinate resident registration and staff review
items.description: Define the first-release workflow for browsing workshops, requesting registration, and letting staff review signups in one manageable process.
notes: Keep the first release focused on the smallest useful registration workflow.

items.title: Let a resident register for a workshop and let staff confirm the request
items.description: As a resident, I want to browse a workshop and submit a registration request so I can reserve a spot without back-and-forth email, while staff can review and confirm the request in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: A resident can browse a list of upcoming workshops with short summaries.
A resident can open a workshop detail view and understand how to request a spot.
A resident can submit a registration request with contact details and attendance preferences.
A staff member can review pending registration requests and mark a request as confirmed.
assumptions: The first release can treat each workshop as a simple availability unit rather than a full scheduling system.

items.title: Define the workshop browsing and registration content flow
items.description: Outline the workshop list, detail, and registration request flow so the resident journey stays clear before implementation work is split further.

items.title: Break the resident and staff screens into front-end tasks
items.description: Prepare implementation-ready front-end tasks for the workshop list, registration form, and staff review screen with clear component and state boundaries.

items.title: Define registration storage and confirmation service behavior
items.description: Describe the minimum data fields and service behavior needed to store registration requests, list pending signups, and update a request to confirmed.

items.title: Prepare QA checks for the resident registration workflow
items.description: Capture validation checks for workshop browsing, registration submission, and staff confirmation so the MVP can be reviewed before implementation is treated as ready.

items.title: Review sequencing and cross-role dependencies for the first release
items.description: Challenge the current task ordering and highlight the smallest changes needed to keep the registration MVP aligned with the documented architecture and delivery flow.

items.title: Review consent, privacy notice, and data retention needs
items.description: Identify the minimum consent language, privacy documentation, and attendee data retention expectations that should be visible before the registration flow is treated as ready.

items.title: Review staff access and attendee data handling risks
items.description: Surface the main security concerns around staff dashboard access, attendee contact data, and misuse of registration review actions in the first release.

items.title: Outline environments, deployment checks, and monitoring basics
items.description: Define the smallest practical operational tasks for environments, deployment confidence, rollback readiness, and basic monitoring for the workshop MVP.

items.title: Define registration funnel events and workshop signup KPIs
items.description: Recommend the minimum events, KPI definitions, and reporting checks needed to understand whether residents discover workshops and complete the registration flow.

items.title: Prepare resident-facing guidance and staff support notes
items.description: Identify the onboarding, help, and release communication notes needed so residents and staff understand the first-release registration workflow clearly.

items.title: Staff may confirm registrations without enough visibility into consent and attendance details
items.description: The first-release staff review flow could lead to incorrect confirmations if attendee consent state, registration notes, or workshop capacity context are unclear at review time.
notes: Review whether the first release needs explicit consent state and capacity warnings in the staff dashboard.
